title: Dogfooding Review: Local Agent And CI Proof Boundary
goal: Review whether the package makes the boundary between local agent proof and CI-owned proof explicit enough for cheap, trustworthy daily work.
scope: Recent commit-hook, proof-selection, and generated-package proof dogfooding on 2026-05-07.
Local proof commands that are expensive, serial-sensitive, or better owned by CI.
non_goals: Remove required safety proof from release or CI workflows.
Choose a new CI provider or change release gating.

review_mode.review question: Can agents tell which proof is required locally before commit and which proof is intentionally deferred to CI?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: commit 85f29f0d, GitHub issue #828, proof routing docs, recent local proof failures and reruns

review_method.commands used: git status --short; uv run agentic-workspace summary --profile compact --task ... --format json
review_method.evidence sources: Current thread dogfooding observations; commit 85f29f0d; GitHub issue #828; current planning state

references.label: promoted local agent versus CI proof boundary friction
tests removed from commit hook and docs proof globs tightened

findings.title: Proof selection needs an explicit local-agent versus CI-owned responsibility signal
findings.summary: Removing tests from the commit hook fixed one concrete overreach, but the package still needs a general way to say whether a check is local pre-commit proof, local closeout proof, serial local proof, or CI-owned proof.
findings.evidence: On 2026-05-07, commit 85f29f0d moved tests out of the commit hook because CI checks are sufficient there. The same dogfooding pass produced #828 because package-build proof can fail transiently under parallel local proof while serial reruns pass.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents may pay for heavy local checks that CI already owns, skip necessary local safety proof because all checks feel equally expensive, or misread transient build contention as a product failure.
findings.suggested action: Create a proof-boundary lane that exposes proof responsibility classes in package proof recommendations and compact closeout guidance: local-precommit, local-closeout, local-serial, and ci-owned.
findings.promotion target: GitHub review issue and roadmap candidate
findings.promotion trigger: Promote now because the user explicitly asked to create issues from the general-improvement cases and prioritize friction-related lanes.
findings.post-remediation note shape: Intent / What changed / Proof / Remaining risk / Durable residue / Next owner

recommendation.promote: Promote the finding to a review issue and prioritize it with other friction-related proof lanes.
recommendation.defer: Implementation can follow active TypeScript work unless local proof cost blocks the current lane.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss; the finding has both a fixed example and a remaining related open issue.

retention.closeout shape: Keep this artifact as compact evidence for the issue and roadmap candidate.
retention.trigger: Archive or distill after proof recommendations expose responsibility classes and local closeout uses them.
retention.proof surface: Proof selector output, planning surface checks, and a focused route fixture for CI-owned versus local proof.
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

drift_log: 2026-05-07: Review created from dogfooding friction and promoted to GitHub issue #829.
2026-05-07: #829 and #828 implemented in friction-lane pass with proof_responsibility, execution_mode, and ci_relationship metadata on selected proof lanes.
